skill.name: senior-qa
skill.description: QA guidance and basic CLI helpers for test planning, coverage review, and E2E test scaffolding in JavaScript web projects.
skill.summary: Plan QA work and run basic testing helper scripts for modern web apps.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, environment file, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and filesystem patterns. Review found the command, environment, cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts are documentation or text false positives. The only confirmed behavior is user-directed JSON report writing in three Python scripts, so the skill is low risk and publishable.

low_findings.title: User-Controlled Report Output Path
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: Each Python helper writes JSON results to the path supplied with --output. This is legitimate report generation, but it can overwrite files if a user chooses an unsafe path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write calls are direct and easy to verify, but they are only reached when the user requests JSON output and provides a path. No hidden file access or exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences and inline documentation for example commands. No Ruby backticks, subprocess calls, or shell execution were found in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is Markdown documentation, and the Python scripts do not import subprocess or call a shell. The static pattern matched formatting, not executable behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines contain YAML metadata, descriptions, argparse setup, and generic reference text. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain no crypto imports, digest calls, or password handling. The reported weak crypto locations are ordinary documentation and CLI parser text.

low_findings.title: Environment File Alert Is Setup Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The .env reference appears in a setup command that copies an example file. The skill does not read, print, transmit, or parse environment secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only environment-file content is documentation and an audit report entry. No script uses os.environ or opens a .env file.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Section Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged reconnaissance lines are documentation phrases such as anti-patterns and real-world scenarios. No host discovery, network scanning, or system inventory logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are headings or descriptions, and the scripts only validate a user-provided path. No reconnaissance command or network API appears in the reviewed files.

content.user_title: Generate QA Plans and Test Reviews
content.value_statement: Testing plans are often incomplete or hard to keep consistent. This skill provides QA checklists, testing prompts, and basic CLI report helpers.
actual_capabilities: Provides QA guidance for test strategy, automation patterns, and best practices.
Includes Python CLI helpers for test suite, coverage, and E2E workflow placeholders.
Validates a target path before running each helper script.
Prints a basic status report with target, status, and finding count.
Can emit JSON status output to stdout or a user-selected file.
limitations: The scripts contain placeholder analysis logic and do not inspect source code deeply.
It does not execute project tests or calculate real coverage metrics.
The reference documents are generic and require project-specific adaptation.
Users must review generated plans before applying them to production workflows.

use_cases.title: Plan component test coverage
use_cases.description: Create a checklist for component states, props, accessibility behavior, and user interactions.

use_cases.title: Review testing gaps
use_cases.description: Use the guidance and helper reports to organize missing unit, integration, and E2E checks.

use_cases.title: Standardize QA practices
use_cases.description: Define team testing expectations using shared strategy, automation, and best-practice references.

prompt_templates.title: Create a QA Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a new feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use senior-qa to create a QA checklist for this feature: [feature summary]. Include unit, integration, E2E, accessibility, and regression checks.

prompt_templates.title: Review Test Coverage Gaps
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of an existing project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use senior-qa to review my project at [path]. Identify likely testing gaps and suggest priority checks for the next sprint.

prompt_templates.title: Design E2E Scenarios
prompt_templates.prompt: Use senior-qa to design E2E scenarios for [user journey]. Include setup data, assertions, failure states, and maintenance risks.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Team QA Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use senior-qa to create a testing strategy for [application type]. Include test layers, ownership, coverage goals, release gates, and review cadence.

output_examples.input: Create a QA checklist for a checkout flow.
output: Critical paths include cart review, payment submission, address validation, and order confirmation.
High-priority checks include failed payment, expired session, duplicate submission, and keyboard-only completion.
Recommended coverage includes unit tests for validation and E2E tests for the successful purchase path.

output_examples.input: Review testing gaps for a Next.js dashboard.
output: Likely gaps include empty states, loading states, permission changes, and API error handling.
Add integration tests for data refresh and role-based navigation.
Add one E2E scenario for the primary dashboard workflow before release.

output_examples.input: Create a team QA strategy for a Node.js API.
output: Use unit tests for validation and service logic, integration tests for database behavior, and contract tests for public endpoints.
Set release gates for critical endpoint coverage, failed migration checks, and authentication regressions.
Review flaky tests weekly and remove tests that only assert implementation details.

best_practices: Start with user-critical flows before adding broad coverage targets.
Separate unit, integration, and E2E goals so each layer has a clear purpose.
Review generated QA plans against the actual product risks before execution.
anti_patterns: Do not treat placeholder helper output as a real coverage analysis.
Do not run documented setup commands without checking local files first.
Do not measure quality only by coverage percentage or test count.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete test files?
faq.answer: No. The included scripts are basic placeholders, and the main value is QA planning guidance.

faq.question: Can it analyze real coverage reports?
faq.answer: Not by itself. It can structure a review, but it does not parse real coverage data.

faq.question: Which projects fit this skill best?
faq.answer: It is most useful for React, Next.js, Node.js, and similar JavaScript web applications.

faq.question: Does it run external commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. The Markdown files show example commands, but the scripts do not execute shell commands.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The scripts can write a report to a user-selected path, but no malicious behavior was found.

faq.question: Can teams adapt the references?
faq.answer: Yes. Teams should tailor the generic practices to their architecture, release process, and risk model.
